description: The last garden constructed among the Suzhou classical gardens, incorporating the essence of various garden styles.

attraction_name: Pingjiang Road Historical and Cultural District
description: The best-preserved ancient street district in Suzhou, featuring Ming and Qing dynasty buildings along the river, with enchanting night views.
A classical garden within Tongli Ancient Town, renowned for its association with the Kunqu opera 'The Peony Pavilion.'
A cultural and artistic district in Suzhou, lined with cafes, bookstores, and galleries, exuding a strong cultural atmosphere.
The core garden of Tongli Ancient Town, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a representative work of late Jiangnan garden design.
A perfect blend of modern architecture designed by I. M. Pei and classical gardens, showcasing the essence of Jiangnan culture.
A symbolic landmark of Tongli Ancient Town, featuring three interconnected stone bridges symbolizing good fortune and prosperity.
A thousand-year-old ancient street with picturesque night views, abundant traditional snacks, and a chance to experience Suzhou's classical nightlife.
One of China's Four Great Gardens, an outstanding example of Ming dynasty garden art, with clear waters and intricately arranged pavilions and towers.
